page: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The configuration includes 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. The processor is equipped with 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 480 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8), and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is provisioned with 8.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core) and includes 118 GB of local storage, with support for Premium Storage. No public IPv4 addresses are bundled.

Benchmark data reveals a performance profile characterized by strong single-threaded capabilities but constrained multi-core and memory throughput. In single-core CPU testing via stress-ng, the server achieves an average score of 2268.08, and its single-threaded gzip compression performance is strong at 30.79 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is weak, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 2300.17 and a PassMark CPU score of 4093.02. Memory performance represents a notable bottleneck; large-block memory write bandwidth is poor at 11.86 GB/sec, while read-only large-block bandwidth is weak at 25.26 GB/sec. Database operations and web serving also fall into the lower performance tiers, with Redis SET operations registering at 4.06 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 4.86 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2ds_v7 offers a balanced entry-level hardware profile with dedicated compute resources and local storage, making it a viable option for workloads that do not require high memory bandwidth or intensive multi-core parallel processing. The inclusion of 118 GB of local SSD storage provides a resource-dense option for applications requiring fast local scratch space without the overhead of external disk attachments. Given the single-core strength and multi-core limitations, this server is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and small-scale development or testing environments.
description: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, featuring 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz, it includes 118 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The hardware architecture features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities, such as a gzip compression speed of 30.79 GB/sec, but reveal limitations in multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth, with large-block write speeds of 11.86 GB/sec. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources and local storage. It is suitable for lightweight web serving, single-threaded applications, and development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 118 GB local storage, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor with strong single-threaded performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a 2-vCPU general-purpose VM with 8 GB RAM, 118 GB local storage, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A dual-vCPU virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel processing, integrated local storage, and strong single-threaded execution capabilities.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory and 118 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-threaded performance with AVX-512 and Intel AMX support
Constrained multi-core scaling and memory write bandwidth
Suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded tasks, and development
